mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market, indicated by declining unemployment rates, increases wage pressures that erode corporate margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 3.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns exceeds -0.2 in 12-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and payrolls signals robust supply-side expansion, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and PAYEMS shows a negative coefficient in 60-day rolling regressions, or SPX returns in high-INDPRO/high-PAYEMS periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong demand signals (rising GDP, housing starts, and real sales) indicate economic expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in periods with rising GDPC1/HOUST/RSAFS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the composite demand index shows negative correlation with SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production reflects strong manufacturing activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth in the prior 2 months shows no significant correlation with 5-day SPX returns (Spearman rho < 0.1) or 5-day returns in high-INDPRO periods are negative.

mechanism_narrative: Weak housing starts signal reduced demand for construction, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in periods with HOUST < 1200 are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.1) or the HOUST-SPX return correlation exceeds -0.3 in 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a stagflationary environment that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows positive coefficient in 60-day rolling regressions, or SPX returns in stagflationary conditions are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by rising federal funds rates above 4.5%, compress equity valuations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.1) or the FEDFUNDS-SPX return correlation exceeds -0.2 in 12-month windows.
